---
title: "Gamma Likelihood Latent GP"
subtitle: "Experiment exp_gamma_likelihood_latent_gp"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
---

## Overview

Prototype replacing the Gaussian/Box-Cox observation model with a Gamma likelihood.
The latent GP mean remains the SWR kernel-convolution structure (K=4, Gaussian kernels, m=20).
A log-Gaussian SWR-GP baseline is fitted with the same hyperparameters for direct comparison.

**Config:** K=4, m=20, ν=1.5, maxiter=202, n_samples=1000, seed=42.

## Test Performance

| Model | RMSE | NSE | CRPS | Coverage 95% | PI width |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SWR-GP log-Gaussian (baseline) | 1.776 | 0.823 | — | — | — |
| Gamma latent GP | 1.776 | 0.823 | 0.271 | 98.2% | 1.890 |

The Gamma likelihood achieves CRPS = 0.271, below SWR-GP-BC kriging CRPS (0.372), suggesting
the Gamma observation model captures predictive uncertainty more efficiently on this dataset.
Coverage at 98.2% is slightly over-dispersed relative to the nominal 95%.

## Notes

CRPS is computed from Monte Carlo posterior samples (empirical CRPS).
The baseline uses log-Gaussian kriging, not Box-Cox, because the Gamma prototype works
in log space. A direct Box-Cox comparison would require a separate run.
